payload.message:
The elevated run confirms the sandbox was only part of the noise.

Real result from `mise run test:e2e:lifecycle` on the host:
- All `pi` lifecycle tests passed, including `InteractiveSession`.
- All `kiro` prompt-driven tests failed because `kiro-cli-chat` is not installed or not on `PATH`.
- `kiro` interactive also failed for the same reason; `tmux` itself was no longer the blocker.

So the current genuine issue is not the harness, it’s the local Kiro dependency:
- [e2e/agents/kiro.go](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/e2e/agents/kiro.go:26) expects `kiro-cli-chat`.
- Without that binary, `test:all` will fail on any machine that doesn’t have Kiro installed.

Given that, I think the repo should skip lifecycle runs for agents whose real CLI binary is unavailable locally. That keeps `test:all` useful without weakening CI, because CI can still install the agent CLIs it intends to exercise.

If you want, I can implement that next:
- skip an agent in `ForEachAgent` when `exec.LookPath(agent.Binary())` fails
- keep a clear `t.Skip("<binary> not in PATH")` message
- leave actual test failures intact when the binary exists but behavior is wrong

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

content.text: what else is test:all running? we are having e2e as manual explicit in the cli repo

payload.message: what else is test:all running? we are having e2e as manual explicit in the cli repo

Right now [mise.toml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/mise.toml:26) has:

- `test:unit` → all agent unit tests
- `test:e2e:lifecycle` → the repo’s lifecycle integration suite in `e2e/`
- `test:all` → depends on both `test:unit` and `test:e2e:lifecycle`

So `test:all` is currently:
1. all per-agent unit tests
2. all lifecycle e2e tests

Nothing else.

If you want this repo aligned with `../cli`, then yes, the cleaner move is probably:
- make `test:all` only run fast/local-safe tests, likely just `test:unit`
- keep `test:e2e` as explicit/manual
- optionally add a separate `test:ci` or `check` task later if you want a fuller pre-CI bundle

That would be a better fit than teaching `test:all` to skip missing agent CLIs. If you want, I can change it that way now.

name: Lint

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version: "stable"

      - name: Check formatting
        run: |
          unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
          if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
            echo "The following files are not formatted:"
            echo "$unformatted"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Run golangci-lint (agent-kiro)
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v9
        with:
          version: "v2.11.3"
          working-directory: agents/entire-agent-kiro

      - name: Run golangci-lint (e2e)
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v9
        with:
          version: "v2.11.3"
          working-directory: e2e

  pull_request:

      - name: Run golangci-lint (e2e)
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v9

          working-directory: e2e

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

content.text: do the agents have integration tests?

payload.message: do the agents have integration tests?

Not in the same sense as `../cli`.

Current split here is:

- Unit tests:
  In each agent module, e.g. `agents/entire-agent-kiro/...` and `agents/entire-agent-pi/...`
- Protocol compliance tests:
  Black-box external-agent protocol checks via `external-agents-tests` in CI
- Lifecycle integration tests:
  Shared repo-level `e2e/` tests that exercise `entire enable`, prompts, checkpoints, rewind, and interactive sessions against real agent CLIs

So the agents do have integration coverage, but it lives in the shared `e2e/` harness, not as per-agent `integration` test packages/tasks inside each agent module.

In practice:
- `kiro` has unit tests plus shared lifecycle integration coverage
- `pi` has unit tests plus shared lifecycle integration coverage
- neither currently has a separate per-agent `test:integration` style task like `../cli`

If you want, I can restructure the root tasks to reflect that explicitly:
- `test:unit`
- `test:integration` -> current lifecycle e2e
- `test:all` -> just fast local-safe tests, or include integration if you prefer

name: CI

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  test-agents:
    name: test-agents (${{ matrix.name }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Unit tests
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh test "${{ matrix.dir }}"

      - name: Build
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh build "${{ matrix.dir }}"

name: Protocol Compliance

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\",\"binary\":\"${dir}/${name}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  test-agents:
    name: test-agents (${{ matrix.name }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Build agent binary
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh build "${{ matrix.dir }}"

      - name: Run protocol compliance suite
        uses: entireio/external-agents-tests@main
        with:
          binary-path: ${{ matrix.binary }}

on:

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then

            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))

name: Protocol Compliance

on:
  pull_request:
  push:

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:

      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\",\"binary\":\"${dir}/${name}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))

    needs: discover-agents

payload.message:
I changed the root task layout to match that split.

Current root `mise` tasks in [mise.toml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/mise.toml:8):
- `test` → default fast suite for all agents
- `test:ci` → currently just runs `test`
- `test:e2e`
- `test:e2e:lifecycle`

I removed the canonical `test:unit` / `test:all` surface and kept them only as hidden deprecated aliases:
- `test:unit` → `test`
- `test:all` → `test:ci`

Docs are updated in [README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/README.md:55) to show:
- `mise run test`
- `mise run test:ci`
- `mise run test:e2e`
- `mise run test:e2e:lifecycle`

Verified:
- `mise tasks` shows the new task names
- `mise run test` passes
- `mise run test:ci` passes

I didn’t change the workflows for this rename because they already call the shared runner directly, not `mise run test:unit` or `mise run test:all`.

One remaining cleanup I’d still recommend: add root `fmt` and `lint` tasks and decide whether CI should call them or keep formatting/lint only in GitHub Actions.

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

content.text: what's the diff between e2e and e2e:lifecycle?

payload.message: what's the diff between e2e and e2e:lifecycle?

Right now there is no behavioral difference.

In [mise.toml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/mise.toml:8):
- `test:e2e` just runs `mise run test:e2e:lifecycle`
- `test:e2e:lifecycle` runs the actual Go test command in `e2e/`

So:
- `test:e2e` is the user-facing shortcut
- `test:e2e:lifecycle` is the explicit underlying target

That mirrors the naming style where `test:e2e` is the broad entrypoint and `test:e2e:<kind>` is the concrete subtype, even though today there’s only one e2e subtype. If this repo later adds another e2e class, `test:e2e` could become the umbrella task.

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 997e3a8..516e68d 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -47,27 +47,30 @@ The skill files live in `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/` if you want to r
 Testing is intentionally split:
 
 - **Generic protocol checks** run in GitHub Actions via [`entireio/external-agents-tests`](https://github.com/entireio/external-agents-tests). The workflow builds each `entire-agent-*` binary in this repo and runs the shared compliance suite against it.
+- **Agent unit/build checks** run in GitHub Actions for every `agents/entire-agent-*` directory discovered at runtime.
 - **Lifecycle tests** stay in this repo's [`e2e/`](e2e/) harness. These verify the parts that depend on Entire itself and on the real agent CLI: prompt execution, hook installation after `entire enable`, checkpoint creation, rewind behavior, and interactive sessions.
 - **Unit tests** live with each agent implementation under [`agents/`](agents/).
 
 ### Running Tests
 
 ```bash
-# Run unit tests for all agents
-mise run test-unit
+# Run default test suite for all agents
+mise run test
 
-# Run lifecycle integration tests from this repo
-mise run test-e2e
+# Same as test, kept for explicit CI-style naming
+mise run test:ci
 
-# Same as test-e2e, kept as the explicit name
-mise run test-e2e-lifecycle
+# Run lifecycle integration tests from this repo
+mise run test:e2e
 
-# Run unit + lifecycle tests locally
-mise run test-all
+# Same as test:e2e, kept as the explicit name
+mise run test:e2e:lifecycle
 ```
 
 Protocol compliance runs in CI through [`.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml`](.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml).
 
+For a newly added agent to be picked up automatically by local root tasks and CI, add `agents/entire-agent-<name>/mise.toml` with `build` and `test` tasks. The shared runner falls back to Go defaults when `go.mod` exists, but the `mise` tasks are the contract for non-standard setups.
+
 ### Lifecycle Harness Architecture
 
 The lifecycle harness auto-discovers and builds all agents in `agents/` via `TestMain`:

 Testing is intentionally split:
 
 - **Generic protocol checks** run in GitHub Actions via [`entireio/external-agents-tests`](https://github.com/entireio/external-agents-tests). The workflow builds each `entire-agent-*` binary in this repo and runs the shared compliance suite against it.
+- **Agent unit/build checks** run in GitHub Actions for every `agents/entire-agent-*` directory discovered at runtime.
 - **Lifecycle tests** stay in this repo's [`e2e/`](e2e/) harness. These verify the parts that depend on Entire itself and on the real agent CLI: prompt execution, hook installation after `entire enable`, checkpoint creation, rewind behavior, and interactive sessions.
 - **Unit tests** live with each agent implementation under [`agents/`](agents/).
 
 ### Running Tests
 
 ```bash
-# Run unit tests for all agents
-mise run test-unit
+# Run default test suite for all agents
+mise run test
 
-# Run lifecycle integration tests from this repo

-mise run test-e2e-lifecycle
+# Run lifecycle integration tests from this repo
+mise run test:e2e
 
-# Run unit + lifecycle tests locally
-mise run test-all
+# Same as test:e2e, kept as the explicit name
+mise run test:e2e:lifecycle
 ```
 
 Protocol compliance runs in CI through [`.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml`](.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml).
 
+For a newly added agent to be picked up automatically by local root tasks and CI, add `agents/entire-agent-<name>/mise.toml` with `build` and `test` tasks. The shared runner falls back to Go defaults when `go.mod` exists, but the `mise` tasks are the contract for non-standard setups.
+
 ### Lifecycle Harness Architecture
 
 The lifecycle harness auto-discovers and builds all agents in `agents/` via `TestMain`:

name: License Check

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  check-licenses:
    uses: entireio/shared/.github/workflows/license-check-reusable.yml@6e5ecde24c20a17b4146250287307718987b7317 # main

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"

usage() {
  echo "usage: $0 <build|test> [agent-dir]" >&2
  exit 1
}

task="${1:-}"
agent_dir_arg="${2:-}"

if [[ "$task" != "build" && "$task" != "test" ]]; then
  usage
fi

run_task() {
  local dir="$1"
  local name
  name="$(basename "$dir")"

  echo "Running $task for $name..."

  if [[ -f "$dir/mise.toml" ]] && grep -Eq "^\[tasks\.${task}\]$" "$dir/mise.toml"; then
    if (
      cd "$dir"
      mise trust -y "$dir/mise.toml"
      env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache mise run "$task"
    ); then
      if [[ "$task" == "build" && -f "$dir/$name" && -z "$agent_dir_arg" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$repo_root/bin"
        cp "$dir/$name" "$repo_root/bin/$name"
      fi
      return
    fi
    if [[ ! -f "$dir/go.mod" ]]; then
      return 1
    fi
    echo "mise ${task} failed for $name, falling back to Go defaults..." >&2
  fi

  if [[ -f "$dir/go.mod" ]]; then
    case "$task" in
      build)
        (
          cd "$dir"
          env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache go build -o "$name" "./cmd/$name"
        )
        if [[ -z "$agent_dir_arg" ]]; then
          mkdir -p "$repo_root/bin"
          cp "$dir/$name" "$repo_root/bin/$name"
        fi
        ;;
      test)
        (
          cd "$dir"
          env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache go test ./...
        )
        ;;
    esac
    return
  fi

  echo "No $task runner found for $name. Add $dir/mise.toml with [tasks.$task] or a Go module fallback." >&2
  return 1
}

declare -a agent_dirs=()

if [[ -n "$agent_dir_arg" ]]; then
  if [[ "$agent_dir_arg" = /* ]]; then
    agent_dirs+=("${agent_dir_arg%/}")
  else
    agent_dirs+=("$repo_root/${agent_dir_arg%/}")
  fi
else
  while IFS= read -r dir; do
    agent_dirs+=("$dir")
  done < <(find "$repo_root/agents" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)
fi

if [[ "${#agent_dirs[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "No agent directories found." >&2
  exit 1
fi

failed=0
for dir in "${agent_dirs[@]}"; do
  if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
    echo "Agent directory not found: $dir" >&2
    failed=1
    continue
  fi
  if ! run_task "$dir"; then
    failed=1
  fi
done

exit "$failed"

## Testing

Testing is intentionally split:

- **Generic protocol checks** run in GitHub Actions via [`entireio/external-agents-tests`](https://github.com/entireio/external-agents-tests). The workflow builds each `entire-agent-*` binary in this repo and runs the shared compliance suite against it.
- **Agent unit/build checks** run in GitHub Actions for every `agents/entire-agent-*` directory discovered at runtime.
- **Lifecycle tests** stay in this repo's [`e2e/`](e2e/) harness. These verify the parts that depend on Entire itself and on the real agent CLI: prompt execution, hook installation after `entire enable`, checkpoint creation, rewind behavior, and interactive sessions.
- **Unit tests** live with each agent implementation under [`agents/`](agents/).

### Running Tests

```bash
# Run default test suite for all agents
mise run test

# Same as test, kept for explicit CI-style naming
mise run test:ci

# Run lifecycle integration tests from this repo
mise run test:e2e

# Same as test:e2e, kept as the explicit name
mise run test:e2e:lifecycle
```

Protocol compliance runs in CI through [`.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml`](.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml).

For a newly added agent to be picked up automatically by local root tasks and CI, add `agents/entire-agent-<name>/mise.toml` with `build` and `test` tasks. The shared runner falls back to Go defaults when `go.mod` exists, but the `mise` tasks are the contract for non-standard setups.

### Lifecycle Harness Architecture

The lifecycle harness auto-discovers and builds all agents in `agents/` via `TestMain`:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `e2e/setup_test.go` | `TestMain` entry point — discovers agents, builds binaries, configures PATH |
| `e2e/lifecycle_test.go` | Shared lifecycle scenarios run against every registered agent |
| `e2e/agents/` | Agent adapters for the real CLIs used during lifecycle tests |
| `e2e/entire/` | Entire CLI wrappers used by lifecycle assertions |
| `e2e/testutil/` | Repo setup, artifact capture, git helpers, and checkpoint assertions |

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to the `entire` binary (defaults to `entire` from PATH) |
| `E2E_AGENT` | Filter lifecycle runs to a single registered agent |
| `E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR` | Override lifecycle artifact output directory |
| `E2E_KEEP_REPOS` | Preserve temp repos for debugging |
| `E2E_CONCURRENT_TEST_LIMIT` | Override the per-agent lifecycle concurrency limit |

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

It only checks the string returned by `generateExtension()`. It does not run the real Pi CLI, `entire`, or the shared `e2e/` lifecycle harness. So it belongs to the per-agent unit test layer, not e2e.

--- .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  test-agents:
    name: test-agents (${{ matrix.name }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Unit tests
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh test "${{ matrix.dir }}"

      - name: Build
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh build "${{ matrix.dir }}"
--- .github/workflows/license-check.yml
name: License Check

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  check-licenses:
    uses: entireio/shared/.github/workflows/license-check-reusable.yml@6e5ecde24c20a17b4146250287307718987b7317 # main
--- .github/workflows/lint.yml
name: Lint

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version: "stable"

      - name: Check formatting
        run: |
          unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
          if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
            echo "The following files are not formatted:"
            echo "$unformatted"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Run golangci-lint (agent-kiro)
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v9
        with:
          version: "v2.11.3"
          working-directory: agents/entire-agent-kiro

      - name: Run golangci-lint (e2e)
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v9
        with:
          version: "v2.11.3"
          working-directory: e2e
--- .github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml
name: Protocol Compliance

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\",\"binary\":\"${dir}/${name}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  test-agents:
    name: test-agents (${{ matrix.name }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Build agent binary
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh build "${{ matrix.dir }}"

      - name: Run protocol compliance suite
        uses: entireio/external-agents-tests@main
        with:
          binary-path: ${{ matrix.binary }}

payload.aggregated_output:
--- .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  test-agents:
    name: test-agents (${{ matrix.name }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: jdx/mise-action@c37c93293d6b742fc901e1406b8f764f6fb19dac # v2.4.4

      - name: Unit tests

--- .github/workflows/lint.yml
name: Lint

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  fmt:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: actions/setup-go@4a3601121dd01d1626a1e23e37211e3254c1c06c # v6.4.0
        with:
          go-version: "stable"

      - name: Check formatting
        run: |
          unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
          if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
            echo "The following files are not formatted:"
            echo "$unformatted"
            exit 1
          fi

  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"dir\":\"${dir}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  lint-agents:
    name: lint-agents (${{ matrix.dir }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: actions/setup-go@4a3601121dd01d1626a1e23e37211e3254c1c06c # v6.4.0
        with:
          go-version: "stable"

      - name: Run golangci-lint
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@1e7e51e771db61008b38414a730f564565cf7c20 # v9.2.0
        with:
          version: "v2.11.3"
          working-directory: ${{ matrix.dir }}

  lint-e2e:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: actions/setup-go@4a3601121dd01d1626a1e23e37211e3254c1c06c # v6.4.0
        with:
          go-version: "stable"

      - name: Run golangci-lint (e2e)
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@1e7e51e771db61008b38414a730f564565cf7c20 # v9.2.0
        with:
          version: "v2.11.3"
          working-directory: e2e
--- .github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml
name: Protocol Compliance

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  discover-agents:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      count: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
      matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - id: discover
        shell: bash
        run: |
          count=0
          entries=""

          while IFS= read -r dir; do
            name="$(basename "$dir")"
            if [[ -n "$entries" ]]; then
              entries="${entries},"
            fi
            entries="${entries}{\"name\":\"${name}\",\"dir\":\"${dir}\",\"binary\":\"${dir}/${name}\"}"
            count=$((count + 1))
          done < <(find agents -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'entire-agent-*' | sort)

          echo "count=${count}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "matrix={\"include\":[${entries}]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  test-agents:
    name: test-agents (${{ matrix.name }})
    needs: discover-agents
    if: ${{ needs.discover-agents.outputs.count != '0' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover-agents.outputs.matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: jdx/mise-action@c37c93293d6b742fc901e1406b8f764f6fb19dac # v2.4.4

      - name: Build agent binary
        run: bash ./tests/run-agent-task.sh build "${{ matrix.dir }}"

      - name: Run protocol compliance suite
        uses: entireio/external-agents-tests@3220ca8cc7ba2fbfc5a951ce4a5937ca1a5ca26e # main
        with:
          binary-path: ${{ matrix.binary }}

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

payload.message: can you give me a bullet point list of things done in these commits

- `05d1915` `Fix stale Pi extension test`
  - Replaced the brittle full-string golden in [agents/entire-agent-pi/internal/pi/hooks_test.go](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-pi/internal/pi/hooks_test.go:131) with required-snippet assertions.
  - Covered the newer `tool_call` hook content so the unit test matches the current generated extension behavior.

- `70715b4` `Discover agent tests dynamically in CI and local tasks`
  - Added [tests/run-agent-task.sh](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/tests/run-agent-task.sh:1) to discover every `agents/entire-agent-*` directory and run `build`/`test`.
  - Made the runner prefer per-agent `mise` tasks, with Go fallback for standard modules.
  - Normalized `GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache` in shared build/test paths.
  - Kept root `build` staging binaries into `bin/`.
  - Reworked [ci.yml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/.github/workflows/ci.yml:1) to discover agents dynamically and run unit/build checks for all of them.
  - Reworked [protocol-compliance.yml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/.github/workflows/protocol-compliance.yml:1) to use the same dynamic discovery/build path.

- `ff75321` `Align task names with CLI conventions`
  - Added `tmux` to [mise.toml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/mise.toml:5) as a tool dependency.
  - Renamed the root task surface to:
    - `test`
    - `test:ci`
    - `test:e2e`
    - `test:e2e:lifecycle`
  - Kept old dashed/unit/all names as hidden compatibility aliases.
  - Switched docs to the new task names in:
    - [README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/README.md:55)
    - [e2e/README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/e2e/README.md:31)
    - [agents/entire-agent-kiro/README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/README.md:128)
    - [agents/entire-agent-pi/README.md](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-pi/README.md:72)
  - Added [license-check.yml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/.github/workflows/license-check.yml:1) using the shared reusable workflow already used in `../cli`.

- `588ad4a` `Pin GitHub Actions and expand fmt/lint coverage`
  - Pinned GitHub Actions and reusable workflow references to immutable SHAs in the workflow files.
  - Added explicit `permissions: contents: read` to [ci.yml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/.github/workflows/ci.yml:1).
  - Split [lint.yml](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/.github/workflows/lint.yml:1) into:
    - `fmt`
    - dynamic `lint-agents`
    - `lint-e2e`
  - Extended lint coverage from Kiro-only to every discovered `agents/entire-agent-*` directory.
  - Kept formatting and e2e lint checks as separate jobs for clearer failures.

# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

payload.message:
Yes. On the intended branch/PR:

- PR: `#11` `Fixing tests`
- There are no top-level PR comments.
- There is `1` review from Cursor Bugbot with `1` inline comment.

Comment summary:
- File: [tests/run-agent-task.sh](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/tests/run-agent-task.sh:45)
- Issue: in the Go fallback `build` path, a failed `go build` can be accidentally reported as success when `agent_dir_arg` is set.
- Why: after the subshell, the following `if [[ -z "$agent_dir_arg" ]]` resets `$?` to `0` in the CI single-agent case, so the function returns success.
- Impact: CI build steps in `ci.yml` and `protocol-compliance.yml` could pass even if compilation fails.

The comment is valid. I can patch that next by preserving the `go build` exit status before the copy logic and returning it correctly.

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

payload.message:
Fixed in [tests/run-agent-task.sh](/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-agents/tests/run-agent-task.sh:44).

Change:
- the Go fallback `build` path now returns immediately on `go build` failure before the optional copy step runs

That prevents the `if [[ -z "$agent_dir_arg" ]]` branch from masking a failed build in single-agent CI mode.

I also sanity-checked the runner on:
- `bash tests/run-agent-task.sh build agents/entire-agent-pi`
- `bash tests/run-agent-task.sh build agents/entire-agent-kiro`

The fix is not committed yet. If you want, I can make a small follow-up commit for the PR comment response.

payload.message: `license_finder` works here, but it needs `GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache` for some modules, and its license names don’t always match SPDX strings exactly. I’m gathering the actual license names it reports so the decisions file permits the right labels, not just the SPDX-style ones in `.allowed-licenses`.

I’m gathering the actual license names it reports so the decisions file permits the right labels, not just the SPDX-style ones in `.allowed-licenses`.

/Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/external-.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
decisions_file="$repo_root/doc/dependency_decisions.yml"

if [[ ! -f "$decisions_file" ]]; then
  echo "Missing LicenseFinder decisions file: $decisions_file" >&2
  exit 1
fi

declare -a module_dirs=()
while IFS= read -r dir; do
  module_dirs+=("$dir")
done < <(find "$repo_root" -name go.mod -not -path '*/vendor/*' -exec dirname {} \; | sort)

if [[ "${#module_dirs[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "No Go modules found for license checking." >&2
  exit 1
fi

failed=0
for dir in "${module_dirs[@]}"; do
  rel="${dir#$repo_root/}"
  echo "Checking licenses for ${rel}..."
  if ! (
    cd "$dir"
    env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache \
      license_finder action_items \
      --enabled-package-managers gomodules \
      --project-path . \
      --decisions-file "$decisions_file"
  ); then
    failed=1
  fi
done

exit "$failed"

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"

declare -a module_dirs=()
while IFS= read -r dir; do
  module_dirs+=("$dir")
done < <(find "$repo_root" -name go.mod -not -path '*/vendor/*' -exec dirname {} \; | sort)

if [[ "${#module_dirs[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "No Go modules found for linting." >&2
  exit 1
fi

for dir in "${module_dirs[@]}"; do
  rel="${dir#$repo_root/}"
  echo "Linting ${rel}..."
  (
    cd "$dir"
    env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache golangci-lint run
  )
done

payload.user_instructions:
# External Agent Builder

This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The current testing split is:

- Protocol compliance in `external-agents-tests`
- Lifecycle integration in this repo's `e2e/` harness
- Agent-specific unit tests in each `agents/entire-agent-*` module

## Available Commands

| Command | Skill file | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and wire protocol compliance plus lifecycle coverage |
| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using protocol compliance first, lifecycle second, unit tests last |

## How to Use

When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":

1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
4. Keep reusable protocol checks out of this repo's `e2e/` directory. Add them to `external-agents-tests` instead.

## Tool Mapping (Codex)

The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:

| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |

## Protocol Spec

The external agent protocol is defined at:
`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`
